chapter_title: Tokens, Keywords, Identifiers, Variables and Constants

sections.content: Every C program is made up of basic building blocks called tokens. Tokens include keywords, identifiers, constants, strings, operators, and special symbols.
Tokens are the smallest individual units of a C program that have meaningful interpretation to the compiler.
Keywords are reserved words in C that have predefined meanings and cannot be used as variable names.
Identifiers are names given to variables, functions, arrays, structures, and other user-defined elements.

content: Must begin with a letter or underscore
Can contain letters, digits, and underscores

sections.content: A variable is a named memory location used to store data whose value can change during program execution.
Constants are fixed values that cannot be changed during program execution.

content: Variables occupy memory locations whose values can be modified.

summary: Tokens are the smallest units of a C program.
Keywords are reserved words with predefined meanings.
Identifiers are names given to program elements.
Variables store data that can change.
Proper naming conventions improve program readability.
